title: Olearia viscidula
taxonomy: Olearia viscidula is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread plant families, with over 23,000 known species. The genus Olearia is a group of shrubby plants native to Australia and New Zealand, with around 160 species. Further classification of Olearia viscidula would require more specific information and research into its botanical and taxonomic classification.

intro: Olearia viscidula, also known as sticky tree daisy or sticky olearia, is a shrubby plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and sticky, glandular leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Olearia viscidula include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
